<!-- GENERATED by awf: do not edit; change .awf/ and run `awf sync` -->
# Authoring AGENTS.md

The agent guide (`AGENTS.md`) is the one doc loaded every session. Follow the [Documentation Standard](doc-standard.md) for how to write; this doc adds what is specific to the guide.

<!-- awf:edit layout: default; create .awf/parts/agents-md-standard/layout.md to override -->
## Layout

The guide renders a fixed sequence of sections:

1. **Working with awf**: awf-given; how the config tree renders the project.
2. **You and this project**: adopter-authored; the agent's standing responsibility.
3. **Identity**: adopter-authored; what the project is.
4. **Invariants**: adopter-authored; the hard rules every change respects.
5. **Workflow**: awf-given; the canonical chain.
6. **Working memory**: awf-given; the session-continuity convention for `.awf/memory/`.
7. **Commands**: adopter data; the handful of commands an agent runs.
8. **Document map**: awf-given; the index into the docs, generated from the enabled set.

Override an adopter-authored section with a convention part at `.awf/parts/agents-doc/<section>.md`, or supply `data` (invariants, commands) in `.awf/agents-doc.yaml`.

<!-- awf:edit content: default; create .awf/parts/agents-md-standard/content.md to override -->
## Content

- **Identity**: one dense paragraph covering what the project is, its stack and module path, its maturity, and who it serves. No history.
- **You and this project**: two or three sentences on the agent's ownership stance, the project's long-term health, not just the task.
- **Invariants**: only the cross-cutting hard rules, one terse imperative line each, with the owning ADR in parentheses where one exists. A rule belongs here iff it is *not scoped to a single subsystem's files* — it constrains a whole cross-cutting surface (every commit's process, all code, all rendered output, the toolchain's own preconditions). A path- or subsystem-specific invariant stays out of the guide, in its owning ADR. The mechanism always lives in that ADR.

<!-- awf:edit rules: default; create .awf/parts/agents-md-standard/rules.md to override -->
## Rules

The guide is loaded every session, so it is held to an extra-terse bar; it pays its word cost on every task. State each invariant as a one-line imperative and let its ADR carry the detail. Do not mirror the ADR ledger into the Invariants section: a rule earns a line only when it applies no matter which files a change touches. Reach a path-specific invariant on demand instead — through `awf context <paths>` and the generated ADR status index where the project configures domains, or the ADR index otherwise — and push anything else not every-session-critical into a doc reached through the document map.
